source.title: Development and characterization of a glycine biosensor system for fine-tuned metabolic regulation in Escherichia coli

initial_request.research_problem: Lack of in vivo glycine biosensors in E. coli for dynamic metabolic regulation and directed evolution.
initial_request.known_context: Existing biosensors are insufficient in number and performance, limiting metabolic engineering applications. Few reports use glycine riboswitches for controlling indirectly related metabolic pathways.
constraints: Must enable dynamic regulation and high-throughput screening
input_data: Natural glycine riboswitches from B. subtilis, S. pyogenes, V. cholerae, F. nucleatum
quantifiable_goals: Develop glycine-ON and -OFF riboswitches with improved dynamic range and repression
Achieve tunable metabolic regulation and directed evolution of enzymes

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Insufficient number and performance of biosensors, especially for glycine, limits metabolic engineering applications.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: No effective in vivo glycine biosensors available for dynamic regulation and directed evolution in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Synthetic glycine-ON and -OFF riboswitches can be developed from natural riboswitches for metabolic regulation and directed evolution in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Proceed to screen natural glycine riboswitches for functionality in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Natural glycine riboswitches exist but have not been adapted for in vivo use in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to test natural riboswitches in E. coli to identify a functional starting point.

evidence.quote_or_span: the existing number and performance of biosensors is far from sufficient, limiting their potential applications in metabolic engineering
the number of currently available biosensors is still limited in comparison with the vast number of metabolite molecules
There is few report of using a glycine riboswitch for controlling an indirectly related metabolic pathway for a biosynthesis purpose
we developed the synthetic glycine-ON and -OFF riboswitches for metabolic regulation and directed evolution of enzyme in Escherichia coli

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis formulated; need to identify a functional natural glycine riboswitch in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Unknown which natural glycine riboswitch will function as an activator in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: At least one natural glycine riboswitch will function as an activator in E. coli with a GFP reporter.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen four natural glycine riboswitches in E. coli using a GFP reporter.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Only the B. subtilis glycine riboswitch functioned as an activator to promote downstream gfp gene expression.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: B. subtilis riboswitch is functional; use it to construct a dual genetic selection system.

evidence.quote_or_span: only the glycine riboswitch from B. subtilis functioned as an activator to promote the downstream gfp gene expression

evolution_trajectory.state_before: B. subtilis glycine-ON riboswitch identified as functional in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a high-throughput selection system to screen riboswitch libraries.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A tetA-based dual genetic selection cassette can be constructed using the B. subtilis glycine-ON riboswitch.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct tetA-based dual genetic selection cassette.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The tetA-based dual genetic selection system was successfully constructed using the B. subtilis glycine riboswitch.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Selection system enables library generation for directed evolution.

evidence.quote_or_span: the tetA gene was fused with the gfp gene under the control of the B. subtilis glycine-ON riboswitch
The tetA-based dual genetic selection system was therefore successfully constructed using the B. subtilis glycine riboswitch

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate diversity in the riboswitch to improve its properties.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR can introduce beneficial mutations into the glycine riboswitch.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate glycine riboswitch library via four rounds of error-prone PCR and Gibson assembly.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Glycine riboswitch library and backbone fragment were assembled together via Gibson assembly and transformed into E. coli MG1655 competent cell after four rounds of error-prone PCR.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Library ready for screening to isolate improved variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: the glycine riboswitch library and backbone fragment were assembled together via Gibson assembly and transformed into E. coli MG1655 competent cell after four rounds of error-prone PCR

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify riboswitch variants with improved ON or OFF characteristics.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FACS and dual selection can enrich glycine-ON and -OFF riboswitch variants with enhanced performance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen ON and OFF libraries using ON-SCREEN and OFF-SCREEN assays with FACS and dual selection.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Isolated 32 ON mutants with higher ON/OFF and 118 OFF mutants with significant repression; top variants glyON14 and glyOFF6 identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize top variants to quantify improvements.

evidence.quote_or_span: Glycine-ON plasmid libraries were screened with the assay of 'ON-SCREEN' and glycine-OFF plasmid libraries were screened according to the assay of 'OFF-SCREEN'
Glycine-ON plasmid libraries were screened with the assay of 'ON-SCREEN' and glycine-OFF plasmid libraries were screened according to the assay of 'OFF-SCREEN'
About 200 single colonies containing presumed glycine-ON riboswitches and 200 single colonies with presumed glycine-OFF riboswitches were randomly picked and cultured in 96-deep-well plates
A total of 32 mutants from the glycine-ON library exhibited higher ON/OFF values than the control
118 mutants from the glycine-OFF library exhibited significant repression compared with the wild type

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to quantify the performance improvements of glyOFF6 and glyON14.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: glyOFF6 and glyON14 exhibit significantly improved repression and detection range compared to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Characterize glyOFF6 and glyON14 with GFP reporter assays.
evolution_trajectory.observation: glyOFF6 showed >10-fold repression and 54% higher amplitude; glyON14 showed detection range increased to 107 mM.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Sequence variants to identify causal mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: glyOFF6 shown over tenfold repression compared with that of glyOFF6 in the presence of 107 mM glycine, and 54% higher than the response amplitude of the wild-type riboswitch
the detection range was increased to 107 mM from 80 mM

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which mutations are responsible for the improved phenotypes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Specific mutations in structural elements (e.g., U120A, C106U) alter riboswitch function.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence top variants and map mutations to structural elements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: glyOFF6 contains G42C, A62U, U79C, A92G, U120A; glyON14 contains A19G, A47C, U73A, C106U. U120A may convert ON to OFF; C106U in P0/K-turn may affect binding.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply improved riboswitches for metabolic regulation.

evidence.quote_or_span: Glycine riboswitch mutant glyOFF6 contained five novel mutations, G42C, A62U, U79C, A92G, and U120A
glyON14, contained the four novel mutations A19G, A47C, U73A, and C106U

The mutations A4C in glyON4, A5G and A8U in glyON13, as well as C106U in glyON14 were positioned in the P0/K-turn, which might influence the glycine binding affinity and thus increase the detection range.
Glycine riboswitch mutant glyOFF6 contained five novel mutations, G42C, A62U, U79C, A92G, and U120A (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: glyON14 validated with improved dynamic range.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can glyON14 enable tunable regulation of a metabolic pathway?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: glyON14 can dynamically control ldhA expression to tune lactate production in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply glyON14 to regulate ldhA in lactate-producing E. coli W105 using different copy-number plasmids.
evolution_trajectory.observation: High-copy plasmid pUC18-GlyON-ldhA gave best gradient response but caused growth burden; low-copy plasmids saturated above 7 mM glycine.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Tunability limited by plasmid copy-number effects; explore glyOFF6 for dynamic regulation.

evidence.quote_or_span: The concentration of lactate in W105-18A harboring pUC18-GlyON-ldhA plasmid increased more obviously with the increase of exogenous glycine
glycine riboswitch integrated into the high-copy-number plasmids had a better gradient response
The strain harboring the high-copy-number plasmid produced abundant foreign proteins, resulting in a metabolic burden for the cells and thus decreasing the cell growth
The expression of ldhA, encoding lactate dehydrogenase, was dynamically controlled under the synthetic glycine-ON riboswitch glyON14 in the high-copy-number plasmid pUC18.

the concentrations of lactate in the strains harboring the low-copy-number plasmid P15C-GlyON-ldhA and pZY48-GlyON-ldhA were not significantly increased when the concentration of exogenous added glycine was over 7 mM

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can glyOFF6 dynamically regulate a gene to improve product formation?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: glyOFF6 can dynamically control glyA expression to enhance serine production in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply glyOFF6 to regulate glyA in serine-producing E. coli E4G2.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Dynamic control restored growth and increased serine production by 104% (660.48 mg/L) with yield 85.40 mg/g glucose.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Demonstrates utility; extend to directed evolution platform.

evidence.quote_or_span: glyOFF6 was fused with the glyA gene in pZY48 to control serine synthesis in a serine-producing strain E4G2, in which glyA gene was deleted

the expression of the glyA gene was dynamically controlled by the glycine-OFF riboswitch, realizing efficient resource utilization and an improvement of serine productivity, while also minimizing the need for chemical inducers
The expression of glyA, encoding serine hydroxymethyltransferase, was dynamically regulated under the synthetic glycine-OFF riboswitch glyOFF6

In this work, the expression of glyA gene was dynamically controlled by the glycine-OFF riboswitch glyOFF6 in the serine-producing strain, resulting in an 104% increase of serine production with a yield of 85.40 mg g $^{-1}$ glucose, and improved growth performance (a maximal OD $_{600}$ of 2.
By contrast, the E4GS strain (E4G2 harboring pZY48-glyOFF6-glyA, glyA gene was regulated by glyOFF6) exhibited a 104% increase of serine production (660.48 mg L $^{-1}$ ) with a yield of 85.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: glyOFF6 shown to dynamically regulate glyA.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can glyOFF6 be coupled to a selection system for directed evolution of enzymes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Coupling AGXT activity to glyOFF6-controlled tetA enables high-throughput screening for improved AGXT variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop high-throughput screening platform coupling AGXT activity to glyOFF6-controlled tetA and perform three selection cycles.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Beneficial mutations accumulated; top three mutations reached 85, 90, 100% by third cycle. AGXT22 and AGXT26 showed 58% and 73% activity increase.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze structural basis of improved AGXT mutants.

evidence.quote_or_span: Beneficial mutations gradually accumulated after three selection cycles
The top three mutations reached about 85, 90, and 100% of the total population by the third cycle
AGXT mutants, AGXT22 and AGXT26 with an increase of 58% and 73% enzyme activity were obtained through the synthetic high-throughput screening platform.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What structural changes underlie the activity improvements?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations in AGXT26 (Arg360Cys, Asp201Gly, Asp341Gly, Ile20Thr) and synonymous mutations affect PLP binding, substrate binding, subunit interaction, and translation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform structural analysis of AGXT26 mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Arg360Cys near PLP site; Asp201Gly and Asp341Gly in polypeptide binding sites; Ile20Thr affects subunit interaction; synonymous mutations may affect translation.

evidence.quote_or_span: Arg360Cys was near residue 158 in the PLP binding site
The mutation Ile20Thr of AGXT26 changed the distance between two subunits
four synonymous mutations (84th, 207th, 217th, 293rd) were also found in AGXT26
a higher-frequency codon (UCA) was introduced into the synonymous mutation at 207th site

7d), of which Arg360Cys was near residue 158 in the PLP binding site, the Asp201Gly and Asp341Gly mutations of AGXT26, as well as the Met38Thr, Lys-51Arg and Arg241Ala mutations of AGXT22 (Additional file 1: Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Successful development of glycine biosensor system and applications.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Non-rational error-prone PCR introduced meaningless/detrimental changes; combinatorial mutants not further combined or individually verified; causal mutations not fully resolved.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Future work should employ rational design or combinatorial mutagenesis to fully resolve causal mutations and avoid detrimental changes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations and suggest future directions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Error-prone PCR can introduce meaningless or detrimental changes; mutations were not combined or individually verified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight need for rational approaches.

evidence.quote_or_span: non-rational error-prone PCR enables us to randomly introduce many mutations, but it can also introduce meaningless or detrimental changes when multiple mutations occur
we did not further combine the mutations, nor did we verify the specific function of each mutation

validation_methods: GFP reporter assays for riboswitch characterization
Lactate and serine fermentation with HPLC quantification
metrics: Serine production increase (104%, 660.48 mg/L, yield 85.40 mg/g glucose)
success_verification.final_conclusion: Synthetic glycine-ON and -OFF riboswitches were successfully developed and applied for tunable metabolic regulation and directed evolution, demonstrating improved dynamic range, repression, and enzyme activity.
limitations: Low-copy plasmids saturated at low glycine, limiting tunability
Non-rational error-prone PCR introduced meaningless/detrimental changes
Combinatorial mutants not further combined or individually verified
